$comment: Seed CPSC knowledge base. Each entry is a structured rule object. Citations are real; scope predicates are simplified for the MVP decision tree. verification_tier: 'official' = sourced from statute/CFR; 'community_verified'/'community_unverified' come from the learning loop.

rules.title: Total lead content limit (children's products)
rules.summary: Children's products may not contain more than 100 ppm total lead in accessible component parts.

exemptions.summary: Certain untreated/unadulterated natural materials (e.g., wood, cotton, wool, some gemstones) are determined not to exceed the lead limit and do not require third-party lead testing.

rules.title: Lead in paint / surface coatings
rules.summary: Paint and similar surface coatings on children's products (and certain furniture) may not exceed 90 ppm lead.

rules.title: Phthalates limit (children's toys & child-care articles)
rules.summary: Eight specified phthalates are each limited to 0.1% (1000 ppm) in children's toys and child-care articles containing plasticized component parts.

rules.title: ASTM F963 — Standard Consumer Safety Specification for Toy Safety
rules.summary: Toys intended for children 14 and under must comply with ASTM F963 (mechanical/physical, flammability, and certain chemical requirements).

rules.title: Small parts / choking hazard
rules.summary: Toys and articles intended for children under 3 must not contain small parts that fit in the small-parts test cylinder (choking hazard).

rules.title: Tracking label requirement
rules.summary: Children's products must bear a permanent, distinguishing tracking label with manufacturer info, location and date of production, and cohort/batch information.

rules.title: Button/coin cell batteries (Reese's Law)
rules.summary: Consumer products containing button or coin cell batteries must have secure battery compartments and warning labeling per the safety standard.

rules.title: High-powered magnets
rules.summary: Products with loose or separable magnets that are small enough to be swallowed must meet flux-index limits.

rules.title: Infant sleep products safety standard
rules.summary: Infant sleep products must meet the applicable CPSC durable infant/toddler product standard.

rules.title: Flammability of clothing textiles
rules.summary: Wearing apparel textiles must meet general flammability requirements (Class 1 normal flammability).

exemptions.summary: Certain fabrics (e.g., plain surface fabrics >= 2.6 oz/yd2, and all fabrics of acrylic, modacrylic, nylon, olefin, polyester, wool) are exempt from testing.

rules.title: Children's sleepwear flammability
rules.summary: Children's sleepwear sizes 0-6X (1615) and 7-14 (1616) must be flame resistant or meet the snug-fit exemption.

exemptions.summary: Tight-fitting ('snug-fit') sleepwear meeting the specified maximum dimensions is exempt from the flame-resistance requirement but must carry the required hangtag/labeling.
